claimText: Madhya Pradesh has the largest forest cover by area in India, followed by Arunachal Pradesh and Chhattisgarh.
evidenceExcerpt: Top 3 states by forest area: Madhya Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh.
examUse: Statement-based questions on state rankings for forest cover by area.
distractorHooks: Swapping area rankings with percentage rankings (e.g., claiming Mizoram or Lakshadweep has the highest forest area)
Changing the order of the top three states

claimText: Lakshadweep has the highest forest cover as a percentage of its geographical area, followed by Mizoram and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
evidenceExcerpt: Top 3 states by forest percentage: Lakshadweep (91.33%), Mizoram, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
examUse: Statement-based questions on state/UT rankings for forest cover by percentage.
distractorHooks: Swapping percentage rankings with area rankings (e.g., claiming Madhya Pradesh has the highest forest percentage)
Omitting Union Territories to falsely claim a state like Mizoram is the absolute highest overall

claimText: India's total mangrove cover decreased to 4,992 sq km, with Gujarat experiencing a decrease while Andhra Pradesh and Maharashtra saw an increase.
evidenceExcerpt: Total Mangrove cover decreased by 7.43 sq km to 4,992 sq km (Gujarat decreased; AP and Maharashtra increased).
examUse: Statement-based questions on recent trends in mangrove cover and state-specific changes.
distractorHooks: Claiming an overall increase in India's total mangrove cover
Falsely stating that Gujarat's mangrove cover increased or Maharashtra's decreased

claimText: Forest Cover is defined as all lands with a tree canopy density of 10% or more and an area of 1 hectare or more, irrespective of land ownership.
evidenceExcerpt: Forest Cover requires >= 1 hectare area and >= 10% tree canopy, irrespective of ownership.
examUse: Definition-based questions distinguishing Forest Cover from Tree Cover or Recorded Forest Area.
distractorHooks: Altering the thresholds (e.g., >= 5% canopy density or >= 2 hectares)
Stating that Forest Cover only applies to government-owned or legally recorded forest lands

claimText: Tree cover consists of tree patches smaller than 1 hectare that are located outside of recorded forest areas.
evidenceExcerpt: Tree cover comprises patches < 1 hectare outside recorded forest areas.
examUse: Definition-based questions distinguishing Tree Cover from Forest Cover.
distractorHooks: Stating that tree cover includes patches of 1 hectare or more
Claiming tree cover includes patches located inside recorded forest areas
